整套首页
unit_22 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_22

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 280 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
The company suffers from an almost total lack of ______: even the most innocuous communications between departments tend to devolve into acrimony.The company suffers from an almost total lack of ______: even the most innocuous communications between departments tend to devolve into acrimony.
答案：C　comity
dissensiondevolveconformity
dissension: standard unit headword | devolve: standard unit headword | conformity: standard unit headword
#2 Sentence Equivalence
The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do ______ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do ______ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.
答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to
yielddeviate
yield: standard unit headword | deviate: standard unit headword
#3 SE
The clients initially wanted to declare bankruptcy, but their lawyers were reluctant, noting that bankruptcy can often be a processThe clients initially wanted to declare bankruptcy, but their lawyers were reluctant, noting that bankruptcy can often be a process
答案：A, F　parlous; risky
regrettableroutine
regrettable: standard unit headword | routine: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) ___________ ; they were widely disparaged for being so(ii) ___________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) ____________.Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) ___________ ; they were widely disparaged for being so(ii) ___________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　puerile; lowbrow; abashed
conceptionshrewd
conception: standard unit headword | shrewd: standard unit headword
#5 Text Completion
His idea to make money by building a drone that could fly from the West Coast to Hawaii while continuously sending back low-altitude weather data is a characteristically (i)______ project. The (ii)______ of good storm-intensity forecasts has huge economic costs, but filling that need won’t automatically generate big profits because the economic benefits are (iii)______.His idea to make money by building a drone that could fly from the West Coast to Hawaii while continuously sending back low-altitude weather data is a characteristically (i)______ project. The (ii)______ of good storm-intensity forecasts has huge economic costs, but filling that need won’t automatically generate big profits because the economic benefits are (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　quixotic; dearth; so widely diffused
generateproject
generate: standard unit headword | project: standard unit headword
#6 TC SINGLE
Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.Confounding the perception of needlework as __________, the Oxburgh embroideries, executed by a sixteenth-century Scottish queen and her attendant, incorporate text and imagery from books by distinguished scholars of the time.
答案：E　a vacuous pastime
vacuouslaborious
vacuous: standard unit headword | laborious: standard unit headword
#7 Sentence Equivalence
For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy ______; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy ______; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.
答案：E, F　simplicity; intelligibility
spinaspire
spin: standard unit headword | aspire: standard unit headword
#8 SE
Despite all the efforts of his admirers to bolster his prestige, the reputation of Alexander Pope, both as a person and as a writer, remains __________.Despite all the efforts of his admirers to bolster his prestige, the reputation of Alexander Pope, both as a person and as a writer, remains __________.
答案：A, E　insecure; precarious
prestigenebulous
prestige: standard unit headword | nebulous: standard unit headword
#9 TC DOUBLE
Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　swagger; pugnacious
pugnaciousshoulder
pugnacious: standard unit headword | shoulder: standard unit headword
#10 Sentence Equivalence
The action in Zadie Smith’s novel On Beauty is mediated by an unabashedly ______ narrator who does not hesitate to inform us, as once upon a time the narrators of novels were wont to do, how we behave in general and how society usually works.The action in Zadie Smith’s novel On Beauty is mediated by an unabashedly ______ narrator who does not hesitate to inform us, as once upon a time the narrators of novels were wont to do, how we behave in general and how society usually works.
答案：B, C　obtrusive; conspicuous
antiquatedwont
antiquated: standard unit headword | wont: standard unit headword
#11 Text Completion
Researchers claim that early mammals evolved a multistep process to synthesize vitamin C in their own bodies but that tens of millions of years ago some primates became so (i)______ eating fruits rich in this vitamin that making their own became (ii)______ . Their physiology (iii)______ their behavior. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.Researchers claim that early mammals evolved a multistep process to synthesize vitamin C in their own bodies but that tens of millions of years ago some primates became so (i)______ eating fruits rich in this vitamin that making their own became (ii)______ . Their physiology (iii)______ their behavior. Consequently, their descendants, today's anthropoid primates, cannot make vitamin C.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　accustomed to; an unnecessary cost; adapted to
aversemammal
averse: standard unit headword | mammal: standard unit headword
#12 Sentence Equivalence
The jazz style called bebop was born and nurtured in New York City, and despite a ______ initial reception, it resonated three thousand miles away on the West Coast.The jazz style called bebop was born and nurtured in New York City, and despite a ______ initial reception, it resonated three thousand miles away on the West Coast.
答案：C, D　confused; muddled
resonateconfused
resonate: standard unit headword | confused: standard unit headword
#13 Sentence Equivalence
An abundance of nutrient-rich pollution in estuaries causes algae to ______, much as houseplants grow better when their soil contains added fertilizer.An abundance of nutrient-rich pollution in estuaries causes algae to ______, much as houseplants grow better when their soil contains added fertilizer.
答案：A, B　abound; proliferate
coalescenutrient
coalesce: standard unit headword | nutrient: standard unit headword
#14 Sentence Equivalence
Upon realizing that the indicators of a stressful situation can be extremely ______, the psychologist reconsidered her claim that a reliable way of reducing stress is to recognize stressful situations and then avoid them.Upon realizing that the indicators of a stressful situation can be extremely ______, the psychologist reconsidered her claim that a reliable way of reducing stress is to recognize stressful situations and then avoid them.
答案：B, E　exiguous; tenuous
unrelentingrealize
unrelenting: standard unit headword | realize: standard unit headword

The current ______ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.The current ______ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.
答案：A, D　glut; surfeit
repackagehodgepodge

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　jettison; no special benefit for
jettison

答案：B　precocity
recount

#18 TC TRIPLE
Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) ________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) _________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.Sharon Ghamari-Tabrizi is not the first to focus on Herman Kahn's (i) ________. In 1968, when Kahn was at the height of his celebrity, a profile of him appeared that suggested Kahn had "a thoroughly (ii) __________ sensibility," meaning that Kahn was uninhibited by conventional ways of thinking about science, alert to (iii) _________ positions that were starting to seem obsolete, continually trying to find new ways to see around the next corner.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　originality; avant-garde; abandon
obsolete
obsolete: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　cumulative; additive

Blank 2

Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) ____________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading
contract
contract: standard unit headword

Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious
shy
shy: standard unit headword

答案：C　inscrutable

Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, “yielded rather (ii)______ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America.” So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)______ if only the public schools did a better job.Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare, even movement from poor to middle class was (i)______. Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 3

hybrid: standard unit headword | shrewd: standard unit headword

Witness the (ii)______ a program that allows us to create computer greeting cards for the entire year in one sitting.Memory-prompt technology such as online birthday reminders does more than enhance our recall abilities; it induces us to (i)______ ever more behaviors to automated process. Witness the (ii)______ a program that allows us to create computer greeting cards for the entire year in one sitting.

Blank 2
答案：A, E　delegate; popularity of

答案：A, F　exhaustive; comprehensive

#54 Text Completion
Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even ______: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage, and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.Consolidating a memory is not instantaneous, after all, or even ______: every memory must be encoded and moved from short-term to long-term storage, and some of those memories are, for whatever reason, more vividly imprinted than others.
答案：E　inevitable

encode: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　waning; declining

dire: standard unit headword

These musicians use computers to (ii)______ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)______ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)______ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)______ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)______ these developments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at

答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with

Instead, his work suggests that the photographer simply cannot ignore so much of the built American landscape but is obligated to (ii)______ what we pass through day in and day out, regardless of (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：B　bemoaned

The public loved souvenirs; the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.

Blank 2
Blank 3

#66 TC TRIPLE
The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring
cumulative

答案：B, F　an unconventional; an original

In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle’s corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i)_______ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.

Blank 2
答案：C, E　unique; can be traced to

Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.Until recently, historians of science treated fundamental research as (i) __________ pure science--that is, both were taken to refer to scientific investigations (ii) __________ technological and/or commercial objectives. Now, however, historians have realized that in the mid-twentieth century the phrase "fundamental research" was sometimes (iii) __________ industrial scientists, who applied the phrase to their own work in order to distinguish it from that of applied scientists and thus elevate their status.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　synonymous with; free from; appropriated by
realize
realize: standard unit headword
#105 TC SINGLE
We tend to perceive the classification of zoo animals into scientific categories such as species and subspecies as ____________, but the definition of many species is still hotly debated among biologists and conservationists.We tend to perceive the classification of zoo animals into scientific categories such as species and subspecies as ____________, but the definition of many species is still hotly debated among biologists and conservationists.
答案：C　incontrovertible
imperative
imperative: standard unit headword
#106 Sentence Equivalence
A closer examination of the author’s footnotes explains the datedness of his argument: he has ignored most of the recent work in the field, drawing instead mainly on research that is now decidedly ______.A closer examination of the author’s footnotes explains the datedness of his argument: he has ignored most of the recent work in the field, drawing instead mainly on research that is now decidedly ______.
答案：C, F　obsolete; outmoded
obsolete
obsolete: standard unit headword

As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) increased. Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) .As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) increased. Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) .
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　conviviality; anxiety
routine
routine: standard unit headword

Because molecules containing unexpected isotopes--carbon-13 in place of carbon-12 in carbon dioxide, say--weigh slightly more or less than their more common counterparts and yield valuable information about chemical processes, researchers are weighing molecules on ever ____________ scales.Because molecules containing unexpected isotopes--carbon-13 in place of carbon-12 in carbon dioxide, say--weigh slightly more or less than their more common counterparts and yield valuable information about chemical processes, researchers are weighing molecules on ever ____________ scales.
答案：B, E　finer; more sensitive
yield
yield: standard unit headword
#109 Sentence Equivalence
The idea that Jimwas ______, that he loved fighting for the sake of fighting, was a mistake, in reality he was a kind and gentle person.The idea that Jimwas ______, that he loved fighting for the sake of fighting, was a mistake, in reality he was a kind and gentle person.
答案：A, E　truculent; pugnacious
pugnacious
pugnacious: standard unit headword

The same relationship between (ii) and (iii) behavior appeared in several experiments. The researchers suggest that being high up, or the mere act of ascending, reminds us of lofty ways of thinking and behaving.Researchers found that people who had just watched film clips of views from an airplane window were much more(i) in computer games than people who had watched clips showing scenes from a car window. The same relationship between (ii) and (iii) behavior appeared in several experiments. The researchers suggest that being high up, or the mere act of ascending, reminds us of lofty ways of thinking and behaving.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　cooperative; altitude; unselfish

Changes in what is considered to be "real" are so __________ that it is surprising when people treat a current view of reality as a timeless truth.Changes in what is considered to be "real" are so __________ that it is surprising when people treat a current view of reality as a timeless truth.
答案：E　common
laborious
laborious: standard unit headword

Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially ______ nature.Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially ______ nature.
答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent

答案：A, D　spurned; rebuffed

Since her death in 1960, Zora Neale Hurston's literary reputation has (i) ____________; Hurston died in obscurity, but she now has the status of a major American author. However, despite her (ii) ____________ oeuvre, the (iii) ____________ her reputation is based mainly on Their Eyes Were Watching God, her second novel, which has stimulated more scholarly writing than all of Hurston's other books combined.Since her death in 1960, Zora Neale Hurston's literary reputation has (i) ____________; Hurston died in obscurity, but she now has the status of a major American author. However, despite her (ii) ____________ oeuvre, the (iii) ____________ her reputation is based mainly on Their Eyes Were Watching God, her second novel, which has stimulated more scholarly writing than all of Hurston's other books combined.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　undergone a dramatic change; substantial; growth of
generate
generate: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　ubiquitous; widespread

But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)______ in ways that do not seem (iii)______ at all. Real things happen here – births, deaths, adoptions, affairs, breakups, commitments, ceremonies, civil union – and they matter.The characters in this comic strip fret about the (i)______ of their “little counterculture lives”, especially when terrible things are happening in the world. But the cartoonist makes their lives (ii)______ in ways that do not seem (iii)______ at all. The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine

Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data

By contrast, shale gas (ii)______ its birthplace, remaining in the source bed whose organic matter produced the gas.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　generated in; never leaves
generate
generate: standard unit headword
#157 Sentence Equivalence
Although the essayist’s arguments did not ______ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.Although the essayist’s arguments did not ______ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.
答案：B, E　confound; perplex
perceptive
perceptive: standard unit headword

The new technologies capable of rapidly propelling the economy forward over the next few decades may have the added of helping the country cope with long-term fluctuations in economic activity.The new technologies capable of rapidly propelling the economy forward over the next few decades may have the added of helping the country cope with long-term fluctuations in economic activity.
答案：A, D　attraction; benefit

incentive: standard unit headword
#159 Text Completion
The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.The essential difference between writing nonfiction and writing fiction is that the artist can (i)______ a completed vision of the world, while the journalist never can, the real world being always (ii)______. Art provides freedom from the bewildering complexities of constant change. Indeed, it is the very (iii)______ of well-wrought fiction that can sometimes make it feel more real than reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　realize; unfinished; clarity
realize
realize: standard unit headword
#160 Sentence Equivalence
Wilson is wont to emphasize the ______ of ants, how ants with full stomachs will regurgitate liquid food for those without, or how the old will fight so the young can survive.Wilson is wont to emphasize the ______ of ants, how ants with full stomachs will regurgitate liquid food for those without, or how the old will fight so the young can survive.
答案：A, B　beneficence; altruism
wont
wont: standard unit headword
#161 SE
The Chippewa regarded their treaties with the federal government as solemn covenants and therefore, throughout the twentieth century, met efforts by governmental officials to __________ those treaties with both open and hidden resistance.The Chippewa regarded their treaties with the federal government as solemn covenants and therefore, throughout the twentieth century, met efforts by governmental officials to __________ those treaties with both open and hidden resistance.
答案：B, E　abrogate; nullify
replicate
replicate: standard unit headword
#162 Text Completion
The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)______ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.The antiestablishment comedy of the early 1960s developed in reaction to an age that was more (i)______ , a time when standing on a stage to make fun of a political leader or government official was a (ii)______ act. In those days, the laughter of the audience was something for politicians to be afraid of, because it signaled a (iii)______ in the public attitude toward the whole political class. Today, every politician is a target of satire and comedy, and laughter has become a reflexive, tired reaction to situations too depressing to evaluate clearly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deferential; radical; profound shift
profound
profound: standard unit headword

The Red Sea town of Aydhab presents scholars with ______: medieval records describe it as a major port for ships engaged in trade, yet today there is no trace of a viable harbor at the site.The Red Sea town of Aydhab presents scholars with ______: medieval records describe it as a major port for ships engaged in trade, yet today there is no trace of a viable harbor at the site.
答案：D, F　a puzzle; a conundrum
trace
trace: standard unit headword
#164 SE
The danger of isolation appears often in Anishinaabe stories but is nowhere more __________ than in the Cree and Ojibwa tales about the Windigo, a terrifying ice monster that is literally a lost soul.The danger of isolation appears often in Anishinaabe stories but is nowhere more __________ than in the Cree and Ojibwa tales about the Windigo, a terrifying ice monster that is literally a lost soul.
答案：B, D　striking; pronounced

答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic

Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)______.Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned
jettison

答案：B, D　dampen; impede

Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______ , that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______ , but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______ .

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

Primates are, for the most part, very __________: walk through a tropical forest, and it is highly likely that if you encounter any mammals, it will be a primate that you see or hear.Primates are, for the most part, very __________: walk through a tropical forest, and it is highly likely that if you encounter any mammals, it will be a primate that you see or hear.

答案：A, E　respected; venerable

Yet the traditional commitment to (ii)______ was not simply (iii)______ : this new genre was expected to capture the essence of historical truth even as it allowed space for the writer’s imagination.Since the 1920s, historical fiction writers in China have emancipated the genre from the traditional notion that (i)______ was the ultimate goal of history writing. Yet the traditional commitment to (ii)______ was not simply (iii)______ : this new genre was expected to capture the essence of historical truth even as it allowed space for the writer’s imagination.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　factuality; veracity; jettisoned

答案：E, F　defied; disregarded

As late as the 1990s, Merry Wiesner Hanks argued that developments during the Reformation and the Enlightenment had (i)______ effects on the participation of women in the field of medicine. She claimed that the (ii)______ Reformation and Enlightenment women left female health practitioners restricted to assisting women and children in an unpaid and unrecognized capacity.As late as the 1990s, Merry Wiesner Hanks argued that developments during the Reformation and the Enlightenment had (i)______ effects on the participation of women in the field of medicine. 答案：A, D　preachy; sanctimonious

答案：D, F　circumscribed; restricted

#182 Text Completion
For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
conceptionprofound

答案：A, F　short-lived; ephemeral

#184 Text Completion
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for

It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.

Blank 3

But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.

Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. #208 Text Completion
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed
replicate
replicate: standard unit headword
#209 Text Completion
In aquatic environments, the herbicide atrazine is more likely to (i)______ developing amphibians when it is highly diluted than when it is much more concentrated, a new study suggests. Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii)______ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.In aquatic environments, the herbicide atrazine is more likely to (i)______ developing amphibians when it is highly diluted than when it is much more concentrated, a new study suggests. Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii)______ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　kill; consistent with
trace
trace: standard unit headword
#210 Text Completion
Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i)______ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii)______ , noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　dearth; demurred
yield
yield: standard unit headword
#211 Text Completion
The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes

#212 Text Completion
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence

project: standard unit headword

Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　limited; extensive contacts; similarities

Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　presented; defining features of; propose

#250 Text Completion
One way to predict the effects of global climate change on an ecosystem is to extrapolate current trend in global change factors into the future. A (i)______ of this method is that its predictions (ii)______ actual observation, but the method also makes the questionable assumption that the future will resemble the present.One way to predict the effects of global climate change on an ecosystem is to extrapolate current trend in global change factors into the future. A (i)______ of this method is that its predictions (ii)______ actual observation, but the method also makes the questionable assumption that the future will resemble the present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　virtue; derive from
drawback
drawback: standard unit headword
#251 Text Completion
Although political events in different countries were not (i)______ in the nineteen century, their interrelationship was (ii)______ compared with the present, when interdependence has become far greater: (iii)______ has ceased to be an option.Although political events in different countries were not (i)______ in the nineteen century, their interrelationship was (ii)______ compared with the present, when interdependence has become far greater: (iii)______ has ceased to be an option.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　unconnected; superficial; isolationism
simultaneous
simultaneous: standard unit headword
#252 Text Completion
Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unimpressed with; terrifying; a commonplace
confused
confused: standard unit headword
#253 Text Completion
China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)______; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)______. This human pressure has (iii)______ the South China tiger.China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)______; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)______. This human pressure has (iii)______ the South China tiger.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　implemented; can grow unchecked; doomed

bypass: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　admirable; unusual

It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation’s banking system is (i)______ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii)______.The national bank has been uncommonly powerful in comparison to its counterparts in other nations. It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation’s banking system is (i)______ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　unencumbered by; discretionary

答案：B　an inborn

#257 Text Completion
Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)______ the (ii)______ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)______, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)______ the (ii)______ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)______, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　struck by; suffocating lack of creative freedom; monitored

Because people expect theater directors to be authoritarians, many were surprised that Clark was so ______.Because people expect theater directors to be authoritarians, many were surprised that Clark was so ______.
答案：C　egalitarian

#259 Text Completion
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate

replicate: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：C, E　fervor; indiscriminate

#261 Text Completion
The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)______, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)______.The author argues that the field of sociology has been overly (i)______, partly because, for many scholars, the edges of the social universe are defined by national borders. In this era of increasing globalization, however, sociology is presented with a historically distinct opportunity to transcend its former (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　narrow in scope; parochialism

#262 Text Completion

The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains—even the air we breathe—seem the norm and therefore (iii)______.Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)______. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)______. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains—even the air we breathe—seem the norm and therefore (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　atypical; permanent; eternal

It reveals as (i)______ and (ii)______ some values that have often been regarded as (iii)______, while uncovering other values that do indeed seem to be universal.

答案：A, E, H　transient; relative; nonnegotiable

#266 Text Completion
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event
trace
trace: standard unit headword
#267 Text Completion
Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work. (i)______ to propose measures that might (ii)______ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii)______ , they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.Books that diagnose problems in democratic governance usually conclude with suggestions for procedural change. By contrast, the authors of this new study have, in their previous studies, always stuck closely to the data in their work. (i)______ to propose measures that might (ii)______ the circumstances they document. In this new study, however, in a break from their usual (iii)______ , they conclude by outlining some possible reforms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　forgoing opportunities; ameliorate; reticence

#272 Text Completion
Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive
jettison
jettison: standard unit headword
#273 Text Completion
Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)______ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)______ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)______ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)______ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　a significant struggle; entails

答案：E　follow
